---
title: ""
---

:::: {.grid .align-top}

::: {.g-col-4}

![Ruby Doeleman, PhD student in economics at WU Vienna](images/Ruby_Profile.jpg){width=210}

<div class="profile-links">

[Mail](mailto:rubydoeleman@wu.ac.at)
[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=ESTk7dQAAAAJ&hl=en)
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/rubydoeleman/) 
[X](https://x.com/rubydoeleman) 
[BlueSky](https://bsky.app/profile/rubydoeleman.bsky.social)

</div>

:::

::: {.g-col-8}

I am a fourth-year Economics PhD Student in the
[Doctoral Program for International Business Taxation](https://www.wu.ac.at/dibt/about)
at the WU Vienna University of Economics and Business.

My field of research is public economics. With my background in both economics and tax law,
my research focuses on combining economic analysis with institutional details to contribute
research on efficient, effective, and fair international corporate taxation.

I hold a BSc in Policy Economics, a BSc in Economics and Taxation, and an MSc in Economics and Taxation from the [Erasmus School of Economics](https://www.eur.nl/en/ese).

I teach the [MSc course Tax Policy](https://eur.osiris-student.nl/onderwijscatalogus/extern/cursus?cursuscode=FEM11040&collegejaar=2025) at the Erasmus School of Economics, and the [BSc course Taxation of Corporate Income](https://vvz.wu.ac.at/cgi-bin/vvz.pl?C=S&LANG=DE&U=H&S=25W&LV=3&L2=S&L3=S&T=taxation+of+corporate+income&L=&I=&JOIN=AND) at WU Vienna.

:::

::::
